body.title: Copilot-like interface for CC/DickSimnel (lower priority)
body.result: closed as noise per Akien review 2026-06-07
body.description:
Build a copilot-like assistant interface (web or chat) that leverages DickSimnel's multi-provider inference + Archivist's graph-based learning. This is a UI/UX wrapper around the recursive inference compiler — turning inference → graph → factory → new agents into an interactive experience.

The system learns from every interaction (DickSimnel captures, Archivist compiles). A copilot interface surfaces confidence levels, graph hit rates, cost metrics, and learned patterns so users see the system getting cheaper and more deterministic over time.

Not needed this week — lower priority exploratory work.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step in sprint (likely devices/copilot/, web_server/, or new device)

**Design rules:** no-sqlite; all graph reads/writes through Archivist; every interaction logged to feed pattern capture

**Scope boundary:** IN — chat UI, inference routing transparency, graph hit/miss signals, cost tracking. OUT — new inference models, graph tree modifications, Archivist training loop (those are internal)

**Completion criteria:** A working copilot chat interface where users can ask questions, see inference provider + cost, and observe graph learning signals over sessions. Minimum: 2 interactions show graph density increasing; cost per task trending down over 10 questions
